headline: Hamaguchi's 'All of a Sudden' Sparks Global Buyers' Frenzy Ahead of Cannes Premiere
lede: Riding the wave of 'Drive My Car' success, director Ryusuke Hamaguchi's latest French-language drama has ignited international distribution interest before its festival debut.
body:
Ryusuke Hamaguchi continues his meteoric ascent in global cinema with his anticipated follow-up, 'All of a Sudden', generating significant pre-Cannes buzz among international distributors.

The French-language drama, starring Virginie Efira and Tao Okamoto, represents Hamaguchi's strategic pivot after his Oscar-nominated 'Drive My Car' electrified international film circuits. Cinéfrance International has aggressively pursued worldwide distribution rights, signaling substantial confidence in the project's commercial and artistic potential.

Hamaguchi's reputation for nuanced, emotionally resonant storytelling positions this new work as a critical darling in waiting. The film's selection in the prestigious Cannes Film Festival competition slate further amplifies expectations for a potential breakthrough performance.

Industry observers note Hamaguchi's remarkable trajectory from respected art house director to global cinema sensation. His ability to craft intimate, psychologically complex narratives continues to distinguish him in an increasingly competitive international film landscape.
key_facts: Stars Virginie Efira and Tao Okamoto
